question_title: Will the interest in “amanda ungaro” change between 2026-04-13 and 2026-04-20 according to Google Trends?
summary: Amanda Ungaro's search interest spiked to 100 on Apr 11 following Melania Trump's surprise Epstein denial press conference and Ungaro's viral threatening tweets. By Apr 13, it has already declined to 39 — a 61% drop in 2 days. The baseline for this term is 0-3 (observed for 16+ consecutive days before the spike). With exponential decay projecting the value to ~1-2 by Apr 20, and even conservative decay estimates placing it well below 36, "Decreases" is the dominant outcome. The narrow 36-42 band for "Doesn't change" is nearly impossible to sustain for a full week during post-spike decay. "Increases" would require a major new catalyst (congressional subpoena, lawsuit filing, new Epstein revelations), none of which are currently scheduled.

factors.description: Post-spike decay dynamics: Value declined 61% from peak (100→39) in 2 days, with ~38% daily decline. Exponential projection gives ~1-2 by Apr 20, well below the 36 threshold for Decreases.
Baseline for this search term is 0-3 (observed for 16+ consecutive days from Mar 24 to Apr 8). Current value of 39 is massively elevated with enormous room to fall.
Historical precedent: The March NYT report caused a brief spike to 3, then immediately returned to 0 for 2+ weeks. Rapid decay is the established pattern.
Active story elements could sustain or renew interest: Ungaro mentioned testifying before House Oversight (not subpoenaed), threatening legal action, broader Epstein investigation connections.
'Doesn't change' requires value to stay in narrow 36-42 band for a full week — extremely unlikely for a viral story in rapid post-spike decay.
SerpAPI measurement uncertainty: Resolution API may differ 1-3 points from Google Trends tool, but at value ~39 with expected decline to near-0, this noise is immaterial.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases (85%), Increases (10%), Doesn't change (5%)**

The key dynamics driving this forecast:

- **Post-spike decay is dominant.** Amanda Ungaro's Google Trends interest peaked at 100 on Apr 11 (following Melania Trump's Epstein denial). It's already down to 39 on Apr 13 — a 61% decline in 2 days. The exponential decay rate projects the value to ~1-2 by Apr 20, well below the 36 threshold needed for "Decreases."

- **Baseline is near zero.** For 16+ consecutive days before the spike (Mar 24–Apr 8), interest was 0. There's no sustained floor to prevent the value from collapsing back to baseline.

- **"Doesn't change" is nearly impossible.** Staying in the narrow 36-42 band would require interest to plateau at exactly this elevated level for a full week — incompatible with the observed rapid decay.

- **Small tail risk for "Increases" (10%).** The Epstein investigation is active, Ungaro is threatening legal action, and she's mentioned being asked to testify before House Oversight. A congressional subpoena or major new revelation could reignite interest, but none is currently scheduled.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=amanda%20ungaro&date=2026-03-21%202026-04-20](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=amanda%20ungaro&date=2026-03-21%202026-04-20) for 2026-04-20 is within 3 of the value at 2026-04-13. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "amanda ungaro",
  "date": "2026-03-21 2026-04-20",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
